message:
fix(engine): store scorecard observations chronologically instead of sorted

Scorecard::record() was inserting observations in sorted order via
partition_point, which meant the ring-buffer trim (remove oldest = index 0)
would discard the smallest value rather than the oldest observation. This
caused time-series drift where long-running scorecards would lose their
low-value history and skew percentile calculations upward.

Fix by appending observations chronologically (push) and sorting a clone
only when computing quantiles in summarize(). The ring-buffer semantics
now correctly evict the oldest sample when max_observations is exceeded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
